task_completion.description: Agent investigates the failure by checking docker logs or inspecting the docker-compose.yml file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker logs' for the exited containers, or a text editor/cat command opening '~/projects/store-app/docker-compose.yml'.

task_completion.description: Agent fixes the root cause by increasing the max_connections setting for the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano or vim) or a terminal command (like sed) modifying '~/projects/store-app/docker-compose.yml' to set 'max_connections' to 20 or higher (originally 5).

task_completion.description: Agent restarts the docker-compose stack to apply the fix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of 'docker compose up -d' or 'docker-compose up -d' inside the '~/projects/store-app' directory, and output indicating services are starting.

task_completion.description: Agent verifies that the stack is running and the API responds with 200 OK.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker ps' with store-db, store-api, store-worker, and store-web in 'Up' state, AND a 'curl http://localhost:8080/api/health' command returning a successful response (200 OK).

task_completion.description: Agent creates the post-incident report documenting the timeline, root cause, and fix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of '~/Desktop/incident_report.txt' being written or viewed, explicitly mentioning the database connection exhaustion (max_connections) and the applied fix.

integrity.description: Agent actually interacted with the Docker daemon to manage the stack.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows genuine output from 'docker ps', 'docker logs', or 'docker compose' commands, rather than just echoing text or skipping the restart step.

integrity.description: Agent modified the actual docker-compose.yml file rather than just stating the fix.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible use of a text editor (nano/vim) or stream editor (sed) altering '/home/ga/projects/store-app/docker-compose.yml'.

integrity.description: The incident report was actually saved to the correct path on the desktop.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands showing 'echo ... > ~/Desktop/incident_report.txt' or a text editor saving to that specific path, not just outputting the report to the chat interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The root cause of the cascading failure is PostgreSQL 'max_connections' set to 5 in /home/ga/projects/store-app/docker-compose.yml. The fix requires increasing this value to at least 20. The required services are store-db, store-api, store-worker, and store-web. The health endpoint is http://localhost:8080/api/health. The incident report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/incident_report.txt.
